02

Project description

02

Project goals — safer travel, beyond line of sight

The accident nobody sees coming: a vehicle braking two cars ahead is invisible to the following driver and to every onboard sensor — until it is too late.

Prevent accidents

Vehicles share what they perceive over V2X — a hazard is detected, risk-assessed, and warned seconds before it becomes visible: time to slow down instead of collide.

Safer travel

Awareness is no longer limited by one vehicle's line of sight — every connected vehicle extends every other's perception.

Effortless user experience

The hazard appears on the in-vehicle display as an intuitive bird's-eye scene — glance, understand, react. No cryptic alarms.

Milestone 1 proves it end-to-end: convoy A → B → C. Vehicle A can never see C (blocked by B). B detects C and relays its perception over V2X — C appears on A's display anyway.

Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
03

Technical approach

03

System design — a full vehicle, fully virtual

The complete E/E architecture runs as virtual ECUs on a cloud platform — no hardware, yet a full-system demonstration.

  • Blueprint / node model: 1 blueprint = 1 car; 1 node = 1 ECU, packaged as a container — deploying the blueprint brings up the whole virtual vehicle.
  • Four cooperating nodes: V2X ECU (communication), ADA ECU (perception + risk), IVI ECU (display), plus a team-built Scenario Player bench node emulating the modem and the world.
  • Fidelity per node: full vECU for the IVI, app-level Linux containers for V2X / ADA — effort matched to what each ECU must prove.
4-ECU system design realizing the cooperative-awareness scenario
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
03

Cloud development environment and starter pack

Development runs entirely on the cloud virtual platform under its blueprint/node model — per node: the virtualization level, the development scope inside it, and the goals to achieve.

NodeVirtualization levelFocus goals
IVI ECUFull vECU — developed fully; the container ships in the starter pack3D display: god view of the 3 vehicles; 2D ⇄ 3D view switching; switching to another app
V2X ECUApp-level (Linux container) — developed in part: only certain layersPortability to different hardware — the developed layers move across hardware unchanged
ADA ECUApp-level (Linux container) — developed fullyStructured, high-performance architecture: condensed messages, low bandwidth, low latency; object detection / AI deep learning is not an M1 focus
Scenario PlayerContainer — team-built, developed fully (bench node)V2X message playback across different scenarios
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
03

ECU responsibilities — one clear job each

NodeMilestone-1 responsibility
V2X ECUModem interfacing (simulated); receives V2X payloads, applies business logic, forwards to ADA; builds and broadcasts payloads from ADA data
ADA ECUDetects objects from video, estimates distance, admits tracks; fuses V2X + own perception in a Collision Risk Assessment abstraction; emits warnings to IVI
IVI ECUGUI shell with buttons + central view; renders the 3-vehicle god-view scene — including the occluded “ghost” vehicle — on warning
Scenario PlayerBench node: emulates the modem connection point, plays back V2X messages at configurable rates, drives every test scenario
Track admission is an explicit, testable state machine — no hidden heuristics
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
03

Designed for what comes next

Every ECU boundary is a deliberate extension seam — Milestone 1 ships the scenario, the architecture ships the roadmap.

V2X ECU · portability

Payload logic lives at the application layer, independent of modem and interface setup — a real C-V2X modem later swaps in without touching the business logic.

ADA ECU · pluggable risk

M1's “occluded vehicle” logic is one realization of the shared Collision Risk Assessment abstraction; intersection hazards, curve blind spots, speed-scaled risk plug in as new modules. The warning message is versioned and typed — new hazard types and criticality filtering extend it, not rework it.

IVI ECU · swappable views

The central view sits behind a binding view-interface seam: 2D today; 3D, live camera feed, multi-app wake-on-warning tomorrow — shell untouched.

Future features are registered, analysed, and traced to the seams above — live detection at 120 km/h, multiple hidden obstructions, single-message aggregation, user-opt criticality filtering, themes.
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
04

Development process approach

04

Development process — ASPICE-style discipline, hackathon speed

We simulate the ASPICE traceability chain end-to-end — every artifact answers “why does this exist?”

Requirement
→
Architecture
→
Task
→
Atomic commit
→
Build + test
  • Requirements are engineered: every requirement passes a feasibility study and carries a measurable KPI.
  • Phases carry contracts: each phase declares its input, output, and binary acceptance criteria — done when the check passes, not when it feels done.
  • Total traceability by construction: every task ID X.Y.Z.W encodes requirement · phase · task · subtask, and every commit carries its ID — any line of code traces to its requirement in one hop.
  • Atomic units of work: one subtask = one single objective = one atomic commit — reviewable code, manageable change history.
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
04

Agentic AI with a human in the loop

AI does the volume; the process keeps it manageable, auditable, and steerable.

Researcherrequirements · feasibility
→
Architectdesign · module seams
→
Plannertasks · orchestration
→
Implementation subagentsone subtask · one commit
assumptions marked (A) for veto user decisions logged contracts ratified at freeze scope changes flagged, never absorbed
  • Non-overlapping mandates: each agent owns its stage — and explicitly may not do the others'.
  • Procedure as code: every working procedure — analysis, design, planning, commit format — is a versioned rule in the repository, reviewed and evolved like source code.
  • Result: AI velocity with audit-grade accountability — every decision has an author, a reason, and a record.
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
04

Parallel by construction — contracts first, convergence by design

Two contracts are frozen before any implementation — the V2X message schema and the TrackedObject struct — so three tracks run in parallel against them, with mocks.

Comms, perception, and display tracks build independently and converge at Phase 5
  • Phase 5 converges them: swap mock contents for real perceived data — integration is a data swap inside a shape that already works, not a rewrite.
  • Payoff: the 1-month timeline holds with slack; teams (and AI subagents) never block on each other's internals — only on frozen, versioned contracts.
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
05

Development planning

05

Six phases to August 19 — five developers in parallel

Phase 0 defines the shared contracts, then three tracks launch simultaneously. Phases 1, 2, 6 start in parallel; the moment phase 2 delivers, phases 3 and 4 develop side by side; every track converges at phase 5 for the end-to-end demo on August 19.

  • Staffing — five developers in parallel: one on comms · two on perception · two on display · all hands on the contracts and phase-5 convergence.
  • Convergence window Aug 10–19: mock→real swap, end-to-end rehearsal, recorded demo run.
Cooperative Vehicle Awareness · Milestone 1 · FPT Hackathon 2026
